live_leader_indicator_vs_oracle.note: live.html btcbar: ▲Up leads iff spot>open, NO clearMargin band; every viewer sees this during the window

settle_verdict_with_clearmargin.note: verdictOf: |move|>=clearMargin -> confident Up won/Down won; else 'too close -> oracle decides'

disagreement_move_bps.note: clearMargin (3bps) leaves confident-wrong calls whenever a disagreement's |move| exceeds 3bps

trade_level_btcClose_vs_oracle.note: bot btcClose lags the boundary by up to ~1 candle; this over-states display error vs the site's settleOf(next-open). Shows how often the stored Coinbase ref alone mis-calls the oracle.

provisional_feed_settles.note: settledBy startswith 'feed' = interim Coinbase call not yet oracle-confirmed; 'corrected' = feed call was oracle-wrong

live_basis_check_2026-07-13.note: Pyth Hermes reachable via stdlib urllib; multi-source aggregate, closer to Chainlink than a single Coinbase venue

fix_spec.money_evidence: settle_pending->official(slug)->winner_of(gamma outcomePrices>=0.99) tried FIRST for every pending trade (bot line 1637); _pnl_for uses the oracle winner; Coinbase btcOpen/btcClose only feed an INTERIM provisional (provisional=True, settledBy='feed (provisional)') that step-1 reconciliation CORRECTS against the oracle (lines 1617-1633; 203 trades explicitly 'polymarket (corrected)' in data). Final settledBy is always polymarket/oracle. btcOpen/btcClose never touch a FINAL P&L.
fix_spec.discord_pings: NO price-to-beat is ever shown in Discord. emit_signal(order channel)=ask/cap/secLeft/driftPct; leader decision ping=drift bps + quote ask1->ask2; settle/would-be pings=ORACLE-confirmed win/loss+pnl. Only Coinbase-derived value in pings is driftPct/driftBps (a move MAGNITUDE for engine logic) and leader50 entry SIDE; its RESULT ping is oracle truth.

options.label: Relabel Coinbase as proxy + band the live leader bar + surface oracle truth once resolved
touch_points: live.html 'price to beat' label ~L844 (relabel '≈ Coinbase proxy')
(c) publish winner_of into state.json + render as authoritative settled result in drawBtcBar/drawLiveTrade
options.effort: low (b: ~10 lines, display-only) + medium (c: surface oracle result into published state)
options.residual_error: strike NUMBER still ~$5-20 off true Chainlink ref (cosmetic); 0 confident-wrong leader after banding (all 19 observed flips <=2.22bps, inside 3bps band); live bar near-flat now shows 'too close' instead of a flippable ▲/▼
options.why: Data show the current 3bps band already absorbs 100% of Coinbase-vs-oracle boundary disagreements; the ONLY exposed confident-wrong surface is the UN-banded live 'leads' bar. Banding it + honest labeling closes the gap with no new runtime dependency.

options.label: Switch displayed reference + leader to a Pyth (oracle-proxy) feed
touch_points: live.html fetchBtc()/strikeOf() swap Coinbase candles for Pyth Hermes
bot feed_* add Pyth as primary strike source (feed.open)
options.effort: medium (new fetch path in browser + bot; Pyth Hermes historical/latest, CORS)
options.residual_error: basis vs oracle shrinks to ~1bps (measured 0.9bps live) but the 3bps band already yields 0 confident-wrong, so marginal accuracy gain ~0; ADDS a live Pyth dependency + failure mode
options.why_not: Not warranted by the data: max observed Coinbase-vs-oracle disagreement is 2.22bps over 863 intervals, fully inside the band. Importing a live oracle-proxy buys ~0 confident-wrong reduction for the settle verdict.

options.label: Do nothing
options.residual_error: live 'leads' bar keeps flipping vs oracle 2.2% of the time (all near-flat, small $), and the displayed number is silently mislabeled as the true line. Tail risk: a >3bps cross-source dislocation (Coinbase wick/outage/stale candle) not seen in this 3-day sample would produce a confident-wrong settle verdict.

fix_spec.recommendation: Ship (b) now (display-only, ~10 lines, zero runtime risk): band the live leader bar with the existing clearMargin and relabel the number '≈ Coinbase proxy'. Layer (c) when convenient (publish winner_of, render oracle result as authoritative for settled intervals). Skip (a) — the empirical divergence does not justify a new live Pyth dependency.
